page: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processors running at 3.2 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 105 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities across various computational workloads. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to strong performance in Passmark extended instructions and encryption tests. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded Geekbench scores are also strong. In memory-bound tasks, the DDR5 memory architecture delivers an average memory score on Passmark, with cached read-only bandwidth reaching 3571.17 GB/sec, though uncached large-block writes drop to 84.16 GB/sec.

For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates strong performance in database operations, achieving 63.12 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and strong throughput in static web serving. Despite lacking a dedicated GPU, the CPU's AMX features enable strong LLM inference speeds, particularly for smaller models, achieving top-tier text generation speeds of 351.99 tokens per second on a 135M model. The instance represents a balanced hardware profile, offering resource density suitable for enterprise applications, web serving, software compilation, and moderate machine learning inference without the cost overhead of dedicated GPU hardware.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processors at 3.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong performance in CPU-intensive tasks, database operations, and static web serving, aided by Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. It delivers strong single-core and multi-core Geekbench scores, alongside highly efficient text generation for smaller language models. This configuration is well-suited for enterprise web serving, database management, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering strong performance for databases, web serving, and CPU-based inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB of DDR5 RAM, and dedicated Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual server featuring 48 vCPUs, DDR5 memory, and advanced Intel matrix extensions for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors at 3.2 GHz
192.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and matrix operations
Strong performance in database operations, Redis SET, and static web serving
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Optimized for enterprise applications, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference
